unmapped.human_action: Maintain watchlist on secondary bottleneck-stack names — VRT, ANET, MU. No new options expression warranted at current scanner levels. Monitor Q2 earnings pull-through language in Vertiv, Arista, and Micron IR for deceleration before hyperscaler guidance. Request scanner resweep post-Q2 earnings cycle (late July to early August 2026) to assess whether asymmetry has opened in the secondary names.
unmapped.machine_action: Track Vertiv, Arista, and Micron IR releases for pull-through deceleration signals relative to the prior quarter. Monitor BIS regulatory docket for HBM and advanced-computing control amendments. Flag if TSM or MU HBM lead-time commentary normalizes below standard procurement windows in any quarterly disclosure. Update scanner sweep after Q2 earnings cycle completes.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Corpus claims sourced from named primary IR filings and regulatory databases at 0.78–0.86 confidence. Scanner output from 2026-04-28 sweep across 47 tickers; 35 contracts passed for the AI Infrastructure theme across NVDA, AVGO, VRT, ANET, MU, TSM, DELL. Decision packet dp_ai_infra_bottleneck_watchlist verdict: watchlist_candidate, execution_state: human_review_required. Format selected as narrative_loud_trade_bad per selection_metadata recommendation and confirmed by peak-hype convergence scores at headline names alongside absence of asymmetric setups in the secondary bottleneck-stack names.
unmapped.position_conflict_disclosure: No current book exposure in AI infrastructure in paper or live book. No conflict with the watchlist-candidate verdict.
unmapped.uncertainty: Scanner data is six weeks old (2026-04-28); current IV and open interest may differ materially from scan output, particularly given the AVGO price move implied by the underlying at $399.35 vs. a 480 strike. The bottleneck-stack thesis depends on continued HBM and packaging tightness — any normalization in Micron or TSMC lead-time commentary invalidates the supply-constraint leg. Export-control thesis depends on BIS enforcement posture, which can shift on diplomatic timelines not captured in vendor IR.

original_issue.falsification: Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Oracle each guide AI infrastructure capex lower for two consecutive reporting cycles while NVDA, AVGO, DELL, ANET, and VRT all report decelerating AI backlog or order commentary — collapses the capex pull-through claim. Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC disclose HBM or advanced-packaging lead times falling below normal procurement windows while gross margin guidance normalizes — deflates the packaging bottleneck thesis. BIS materially relaxes advanced-computing or HBM controls for China, or major accelerator and memory suppliers report no China-control impact for two consecutive reporting periods — removes the export-control compounding claim. Hyperscaler cluster architectures shift away from the public-vendor bottleneck stack, with custom silicon, internal networking, or alternative accelerators reducing attach rates for NVDA, AVGO, ANET, MU, or VRT.

title: All the Bottlenecks, None of the Asymmetry
summary: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives.

claims.statement: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives. The decision packet correctly calls this a watchlist, not a trade.
claims.falsification: Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Oracle each guide AI infrastructure capex lower for two consecutive reporting cycles while NVDA, AVGO, DELL, ANET, and VRT all report decelerating AI backlog or order commentary — collapses the capex pull-through claim. Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC disclose HBM or advanced-packaging lead times falling below normal procurement windows while gross margin guidance normalizes — deflates the packaging bottleneck thesis. BIS materially relaxes advanced-computing or HBM controls for China, or major accelerator and memory suppliers report no China-control impact for two consecutive reporting periods — removes the export-control compounding claim. Hyperscaler cluster architectures shift away from the public-vendor bottleneck stack, with custom silicon, internal networking, or alternative accelerators reducing attach rates for NVDA, AVGO, ANET, MU, or VRT.

render.body: All the Bottlenecks, None of the Asymmetry

render.heading: Thesis
render.body: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives. The decision packet correctly calls this a watchlist, not a trade.

render.heading: Signals
render.body:
Hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in vendor IR ahead of hyperscaler guidance: Accelerator, custom silicon, AI server OEM, cloud networking, and power/cooling vendor disclosures capture capex commitments before they appear in hyperscaler earnings guidance, making the secondary-layer IR a leading proxy for the infrastructure cycle. Primary sourced from NVDA, AVGO, DELL, and VRT quarterly filings and IR. (strength: 0.78)

BIS controls as recurring product-mix and revenue-risk variable across successive product generations: Advanced-computing, HBM, and semiconductor equipment controls make China access a compounding constraint — not a one-time write-down — for accelerator, memory, and foundry suppliers. NVDA, MU, and TSM quarterly guidance routinely includes China-control adjustments. Primary BIS regulatory databases and named company IR. (strength: 0.86)

HBM supply and advanced packaging as structural constraint on accelerator shipments: HBM allocation lead times and CoWoS-equivalent advanced packaging windows remain above standard procurement horizons. Documented in Micron and TSMC quarterly calls and SemiAnalysis primary research. Every leading GPU and custom-silicon design requires dense memory stacks and high-yield package integration. (strength: 0.82)

Peak-hype convergence at headline names; bottleneck-stack names at LOW convergence or absent: NVDA convergence 0.80 peak-hype (top tier, 35 contracts across theme). AVGO convergence 0.62 peak-hype, top asymmetry score 9.15 on 480-strike call 20.2% OTM with 30 DTE at $399.35 underlying — deep-OTM momentum expression, not a bottleneck thesis. TSM LOW convergence 0.25, asymmetry 2.43–2.98 across four contracts. VRT MEDIUM (0.50) with no passing contracts. Scan date 2026-04-28 — six-week lag; IV levels may be stale, tier rankings directionally valid. (strength: 0.9)

render.heading: What would falsify it
render.body: Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Oracle each guide AI infrastructure capex lower for two consecutive reporting cycles while NVDA, AVGO, DELL, ANET, and VRT all report decelerating AI backlog or order commentary — collapses the capex pull-through claim. Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC disclose HBM or advanced-packaging lead times falling below normal procurement windows while gross margin guidance normalizes — deflates the packaging bottleneck thesis. BIS materially relaxes advanced-computing or HBM controls for China, or major accelerator and memory suppliers report no China-control impact for two consecutive reporting periods — removes the export-control compounding claim. Hyperscaler cluster architectures shift away from the public-vendor bottleneck stack, with custom silicon, internal networking, or alternative accelerators reducing attach rates for NVDA, AVGO, ANET, MU, or VRT.
